obligations.reason: The scope exposes SimJTAG/dmi_jtag/SimDTM instances and muxes jtag_req_valid or dmi_req_valid into debug_req_valid; visible assignments show selection by jtag_enable but not an authorization condition. Need check whether this is only testbench behavior or a reachable debug subject to protected state, and whether mediation exists at the request source or sink.
obligations.subject: JTAG or DMI requester via SimJTAG/dmi_jtag/SimDTM
obligations.operation: issue debug/DMI read or write and receive response
obligations.object: debug module/DMI registers and any memory/system state reachable through Debug address region
obligations.expected_guard: debug authorization or explicit enable/lifecycle gating in addition to jtag_enable selection before debug requests can affect protected debug module state or system memory
obligations.candidate_path: ariane_testharness JTAG/DTM debug mux -> debug_req/debug_resp -> SoC debug/DMI path
required_evidence_slots: protected object or debug address region
source locations for mux and sink
knowledge_used: evidence closure requires subject, operation, object, guard, path
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows harness wiring but not downstream debug module internals or jtag_enable declaration/source.

obligations.reason: connectivity_mapping computes connectivity_map_o from access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i] but has a special-case alias where target j==6 also permits access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i]. Check whether this intentionally aliases PLIC and CLINT or grants access to one protected interrupt-controller region under the other's permission.
obligations.subject: AXI manager selected by index i and current priv_lvl_i
obligations.operation: route AXI read/write request from manager to subordinate
obligations.object: protected subordinate address regions including AES, ROM2/ROM, Debug, CLINT/PLIC, DRAM
obligations.expected_guard: connectivity for each manager/subordinate must be gated by the correct privilege-indexed access_ctrl_i entry, with no unintended alias between protected targets
obligations.candidate_path: axi_node_intf_wrap access_ctrl_i/priv_lvl_i -> connectivity_map_o -> AXI routing to subordinate ports
state_conditions_to_check: all privilege levels indexed by priv_lvl_i
obligations.uncertainty: The visible fact layer gives enum indices and mapping expression but not full access_ctrl initialization/update policy or final router response behavior.

obligations.reason: The wrapper forwards start/end addresses and sets s_valid_rule to all ones. Check whether invalid/unused rules or overlapping address ranges can route to protected regions without an explicit bounds/validity guard, especially with address bases listed in ariane_soc_pkg.
obligations.subject: AXI master issuing AW/AR transactions
obligations.operation: decode and route read/write address to subordinate region
obligations.object: AXI subordinate address map and protected memory/MMIO regions
obligations.expected_guard: address-range/rule validity should exclude unmapped or protected ranges unless corresponding access control and privilege checks permit the target
obligations.candidate_path: axi_node_intf_wrap start_addr_i/end_addr_i and s_valid_rule='1 -> AXI node address decode/rules
obligations.uncertainty: Full parameter values/end addresses and axi_node internal behavior are not present in the facts.

obligations.reason: rom2 names an internal secure_reg and resets it from mem, but the always block preview shows writes to secure_reg[addr] when req_i and we_i are asserted. Check if the surrounding bus wrapper prevents unauthorized writes, or if ROM2 is writable without privilege/lock/lifecycle mediation.
obligations.subject: bus requester connected to rom2 port
obligations.operation: write secure_reg through req_i && we_i, and read rdata_o through req_i && !we_i
obligations.object: rom2 secure_reg memory initialized from mem
obligations.expected_guard: ROM2 protected contents should be read-only or writes should be mediated by privilege/access-control/lifecycle guard; reads should be mediated if contents are secret/protected
state_conditions_to_check: addr_i index in and out of range
read after write visibility on rdata_o
required_evidence_slots: observed guard in rom2 or wrapper
obligations.uncertainty: Connection of rom2 instance to top-level bus and intended protection status of contents need confirmation.

obligations.reason: Read data is bounded by raddr_q < RomSize, but the write preview shows direct secure_reg[index] <= wdata_i with no visible in-module bounds check. Check whether index width makes out-of-range impossible or whether bus address filtering is relied on for permission and safety.
obligations.subject: requester driving rom2 addr_i/we_i
obligations.object: secure_reg memory array and adjacent state/raddr_q
obligations.expected_guard: writes to ROM2 memory should be bounded to RomSize or rejected/ignored for out-of-range addresses, with no corruption of adjacent state
obligations.candidate_path: rom2 addr_i index -> secure_reg write without visible bounds check
state_conditions_to_check: sim/synthesis handling of out-of-range array index
obligations.uncertainty: RomSize parameter and array declaration lines are not fully included in provided facts.

obligations.reason: The fact layer shows access_ctrl[i][j] sliced from access_ctrl_reg and consumed by connectivity_mapping with priv_lvl_i. Because this matrix mediates AXI connectivity, check all write/update/reset paths for access_ctrl_reg and whether only authorized privilege/lifecycle states can loosen permissions.
obligations.subject: software-visible bus master or configuration logic updating access_ctrl_reg
obligations.expected_guard: software-visible or configurable access-control policy registers must only be writable by authorized privileged subject and have safe reset defaults
state_conditions_to_check: writes while lower privilege mode active
ability to clear/loosen restrictions after lock if any
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists slicing but not declarations, reset, or update always blocks for access_ctrl_reg.

obligations.reason: The access-control matrix indexes permissions by a single priv_lvl signal assigned from priv_lvl_processor. Check whether this privilege is associated with the actual AXI requester/transaction and cannot be stale or incorrect for debug/DMA/peripheral managers.
obligations.subject: AXI requester whose route is mediated by priv_lvl_i
obligations.operation: select permission bit based on priv_lvl_i during routing
obligations.object: privilege-indexed access decision for all AXI routes
obligations.expected_guard: privilege level used for fabric mediation must be trustworthy, synchronized with the requesting master, and not shared incorrectly across concurrent/requesting subjects
state_conditions_to_check: debug requests active while priv_lvl reflects CPU mode
obligations.uncertainty: Only one priv_lvl source is visible; full set of masters and timing of permission evaluation are not in facts.

obligations.reason: axi_node_intf_wrap has test_en_i input and the harness assigns test_en=1'b0. Check internal uses of test_en_i in instantiated node/router and whether any non-harness integration could assert it to bypass permission checks.
obligations.subject: test interface or integration signal driving test_en_i
obligations.operation: enable test behavior or bypass normal mediation
obligations.object: AXI routing/access-control checks and protected subordinate regions
obligations.expected_guard: test-mode signals should be tied off or authenticated/lifecycle-gated in production and must not bypass access-control routing or expose protected data
obligations.candidate_path: test_en_i/test_en -> axi_node_intf_wrap and harness test/debug logic
state_conditions_to_check: test_en_i asserted during reset and normal operation
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer does not show all internal uses of test_en_i or downstream instantiated modules.
